risks: No managed identity configured on Container App — credential management burden for Cosmos DB and OpenAI access
No Key Vault resource for centralized secret management — secrets risk exposure in app configuration
No VNet integration or private networking — all resources are publicly accessible
Single-region Cosmos DB deployment with no failover replica configured
No autoscaling rules defined on the Container App for handling traffic spikes
gpt-35-turbo model version 0301 is legacy; newer model versions offer improved capabilities
No Application Insights resource for end-to-end distributed tracing and deep telemetry
correctionsRecommended: Add a user-assigned managed identity to the Container App and configure RBAC for Cosmos DB and Azure OpenAI access
Add an Azure Key Vault resource and store connection strings, API keys, and secrets there instead of in app configuration
Consider adding VNet integration with private endpoints for Cosmos DB and Azure OpenAI to restrict public access
Define Container App scaling rules (HTTP or custom) to handle variable request loads
Add an Application Insights resource connected to the Log Analytics workspace for distributed tracing
Upgrade gpt-35-turbo deployment to a newer model version (e.g., 0613 or later)
Add resource tags across all resources for cost tracking and operational governance
Enable CORS policy on the Container App ingress configuration
